Protein names: DNA methyltransferase A (DnmA) (EC 2.1.1.72) (Modification methylase M.Bsu3610I) (Modification methylase M.BsuPY79I) (Type II restriction enzyme and methyltransferase RM.BsuMORF6760P) (RM.BsuMORF6760P)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a 2'-deoxyadenosine in DNA + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = an N(6)-methyl-2'-deoxyadenosine in DNA + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:15197, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:12418, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:12419, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:90615, ChEBI:CHEBI:90616; EC=2.1.1.72; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:32324221};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Recognizes the double-stranded sequence 5'-GACGAG-3' and methylates A-5, yielding m6A. m6A methylation functions as a transcriptional modifier, promoting transcription of a number of genes (at least scpA, hbs, rnhC, yumC and zapA). One studied mechanism is via transcriptional repressor ScoC (also called hpr) which binds to non-methylated scpA promoter; when the m6A target is methylated ScoC no longer binds and scpA transcription is up-regulated. Other mechanisms for gene expression regulation probably exist. Binds DNA with and without the target sequence. Although it resembles a restriction-modification system, it does not have detectable endonuclease activity under tested conditions (PubMed:32324221). A gamma subtype methylase (PubMed:12654995). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:32324221, ECO:0000303|PubMed:12654995}.

Organism: Methanocaldococcus jannaschii (strain ATCC 43067 / DSM 2661 / JAL-1 / JCM 10045 / NBRC 100440) (Methanococcus jannaschii)
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a 2'-deoxyadenosine in DNA + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = an N(6)-methyl-2'-deoxyadenosine in DNA + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:15197, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:12418, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:12419, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:90615, ChEBI:CHEBI:90616; EC=2.1.1.72;
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: A methylase that recognizes the double-stranded sequence 5'-GTNNAC-3', methylates A-5 on both strands, and protects the DNA from cleavage by the MjaIV endonuclease. {ECO:0000303|PubMed:12654995}.
